blurb:
Let us live with Christ: words small enough to fit a child’s hand, large enough to cradle all of life.

The lesson is disarmingly simple—where Christ walks, earth becomes an altar, and every breath is a sacrifice of consent. When your silent prayer today overlaps the traffic’s noise or the laundry’s hum, remember that living "with" is more geography than sentiment: it is presence kept honest by his heartbeat beside yours.

verses.text: If then those who had walked in ancient practices attained unto newness of hope, no longer observing sabbaths but fashioning their lives after the Lord’s day, on which our life also arose through Him and through His death which some men deny—a mystery whereby we attained unto belief, and for this cause we endure patiently, that we may be found disciples of Jesus Christ our only teacher.
If this be so, how shall we be able to live apart from Him?
